items.title: CCM welcomes revised Selangor guidelines on non-Muslim houses of worship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Council of Churches Malaysia (CCM) has welcomed the newly revised building guidelines for non-Muslim houses of worship in Selangor, which require large new township developments to allocate dedicated land for such facilities. CCM said the updates reflected feedback from stakeholders and faith-based groups gathered during meetings with the state government. According to CCM, the revised guidelines were presented during the third meeting between the Selangor State Executive Council (Exco) and leaders of various faith bodies on Monday (June 22).

items.title: Diplomacy with all countries secures long-term energy supply for transport, logistics – Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Long-term supply agreements for oil, gas and diesel lasting at least 20 years will provide stability to Malaysia's logistics and transport sectors, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said energy security was essential for both sectors, which depend on a steady and predictable supply to ensure uninterrupted operations, adding that the government was prioritising supply stability amid shifting global conditions, supported by Malaysia's neutral foreign policy and engagement with major powers. Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said strategic cooperation with Russia could help secure Malaysia's long-term energy needs, adding that Russian President Vladimir Putin had indicated readiness for a 20-year oil, gas and diesel supply arrangement.

items.title: Six men arrested after temple machete attack in Jelutong leaves two victims with severed fingers
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 23 — Six men were arrested on suspicions of attacking two men and causing severe injuries at a temple in Jelutong early Sunday morning. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the 4.30am attack was believed to have been caused by a misunderstanding between a victim and a group of suspects at an entertainment outlet, who then followed the victim to the temple. “The suspects are believed to have followed the victim to a temple in Jelutong before attacking him.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 23, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed lower on Tuesday, in line with regional markets and Wall Street. Investor sentiment remains cautious following uncertainty over efforts to secure a lasting ceasefire between the US and Iran weighed on risk sentiment. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded lower at RM260,135.

items.title: Lazada cuts jobs by 5.0pct amid rising competition: Report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lazada has retrenched about five per cent of its workforce across Southeast Asia amid growing competition and a broader push towards operational efficiency. A Lazada spokesperson told the media that the company was reviewing selected roles across Southeast Asia. The retrenchment comes amid wider restructuring across the e-commerce sector.

items.title: Onn Hafiz allegedly barred from entering Felda scheme for land title event
items.summary: KLUANG: Caretaker Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi claims he was barred from entering a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers two days ago. Felda is Johor's golden child. "I wanted to meet Felda settlers across all 73 land schemes, including friends in the Sembrong parliamentary constituency, Felda Bukit Tongkat and Felda Ulu Belitong.

items.title: Altantuya family’s judicial review fixed Oct 6, seeks police action on Azilah sworn statements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The High Court today set Oct 6 to hear the judicial review application by the family of the late Altantuya Shaariibuu for the police to investigate the additional affidavit and sworn statement of a former police officer involved in the murder of the Mongolian model. Shaariibuu filed the application naming the Inspector-General of Police, the director of the Criminal Investigation Department, the Royal Malaysian Police, the Attorney-General and the Malaysian Government as respondents. The supplementary affidavit dated Sept 24, 2024 and the sworn statement dated Oct 17, 2019 were used by the former police officer to support the application for a review of his death sentence in the Federal Court on Oct 10, 2024.

items.title: Masjid Warisan construction in front of TRX tower to go ahead, minister says long‑standing plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Construction of Masjid Warisan in front of the Tun Razak Exchange (TRX) tower will proceed as planned, says Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. He said those with concerns regarding the mosque’s construction could approach the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP) for further explanation. Zulkifli earlier presented appointment letters to the Federal Territories Mosque and Friday prayer surau committee for the 2026-2029 term at Masjid Al-Khadijah, Kampung Pantai Dalam.

items.title: Drug-positive driver jailed after ramming police ASP at roadblock
items.summary: KUCHING: A 29-year-old local man was sentenced to jail after being found guilty of ramming a roadblock and hitting a senior police officer on duty during an integrated crime prevention operation at Jalan Padungan here on Saturday. Kuching district police chief ACP Alexson Naga Chabu said the accused was arraigned in the Kuching Court yesterday and sentenced to six months' jail under Section 26(2) of the Police Act 1967, one year's jail under Section 332 of the Penal Code, and nine months' jail under Section 186 of the same Code. Alexson said the suspect will also be charged under Section 44(1A)(a) of the Road Transport Act 1987 at a later date.

items.title: New York Mayor Mamdani endorsements test Democrats' appetite for far-left candidates
items.summary: NEW York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's push to remake the Democratic Party into a democratic socialist powerhouse faces a primary election test on Tuesday. The mayor, who shocked the political world with his 2025 election, is backing a slate of fellow democratic socialist candidates running in Tuesday's New York primaries as they take on establishment Democrats, including one senior Latino in the US House of Representatives. Furthermore, a democratic socialist won the Seattle mayor's race last year.

items.title: PM Anwar approves RM22m allocation to arm border agency after Bukit Kayu Hitam shooting sparks urgency
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has approved an allocation of RM22 million to equip the Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) with firearms and other suitable equipment, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said the move was agreed to by the Prime Minister following the urgent need to strengthen the safety of AKPS personnel after a shooting incident involving a vehicle carrying one of the agency’s commanders in Bukit Kayu Hitam, Kedah, in February. He said that although AKPS comprises personnel from various agencies, including the Ministry of Health, only certain segments within the agency possess the necessary skills to handle firearms, such as police personnel.

items.title: Ex-Melaka officer charged with fraud and cheating exco members
items.summary: MELAKA: A former senior state civil servant was hauled to the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today to face seven counts of misappropriation of funds and cheating involving more than RM60,000. D. Puhnitha Devi, 43, the former Assistant Senior State Secretary of the Corporate Communications Division at the Melaka Chief Minister's Department, pleaded not guilty to all charges before Judge Elesabet Paya Wan. On the first three counts, Puhnitha Devi was charged with dishonest misappropriation of funds.

items.title: US, Israeli relations under strain as Israelis worry over Iran deal
items.summary: TEL AVIV: American allies of President Donald Trump this week defended him to an Israeli public anxious about a US interim deal with Iran and White House criticism that together appeared to signal fissures in Israel's decades-old alliance with Washington. They sense the US alliance - long the bedrock of Israel's strategic approach - is under strain as opinion polls show Americans increasingly unhappy with Israel and their strongest champion in Washington appears to be turning away. "The United States and Israel have an unbreakable bond," Mike Huckabee, the US ambassador to Israel, said on Sunday after acknowledging there was an "enormous level of anxiety about the relationship."

items.title: Family feud turns violent as younger brother allegedly slashes 65‑year‑old sibling at Air Itam durian orchard
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 23 — A 65-year-old man was seriously injured after allegedly being slashed by his younger brother at an orchard house in Paya Terubong, Air Itam, on Sunday. Initial investigations found that the victim had visited the durian orchard and discovered that several items in the orchard house had been damaged. The suspect then allegedly took a machete and slashed the victim three times before fleeing the scene,” Abdul Rozak said in a statement today.

items.title: Bill seeks to expand law on child sexual offences committed abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sexual Offences against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026, which seeks to broaden the extraterritorial reach of Malaysia's child sexual offence laws, was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. Based on the blue bill book, the proposed amendments aim to strengthen the Sexual Offences against Children Act 2017 by expanding its application to offences committed outside Malaysia by individuals with a connection to Malaysia. Under the current law, the Act applies only to offences committed abroad by Malaysian citizens against any child.

items.title: Prisons Bill proposes electronic prisoner tagging, volunteer rehab scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Prisons (Amendment) Bill 2026, which proposes the use of electronic monitoring devices for prisoners and the introduction of volunteers to support rehabilitation programmes, was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the Blue Bill Book, the proposed new Section 66A seeks to empower the Prisons Commissioner-General to appoint volunteers to assist prison officers in conducting rehabilitation programmes under the Prisons Act 1995. The bill also proposes a new provision empowering the Commissioner-General to order the installation of electronic monitoring devices on any prisoner, including those in custody or those released on licence or parole.

items.title: Bercham storm leaves 460 families counting losses topping RM1.2 million, state agencies rush aid
items.summary: IPOH, June 23 — The storm that struck several areas in Bercham on June 19, affecting about 460 families, has caused over RM1.2 million in estimated losses. Bercham state assemblyman Ong Boon Piow said the estimate was based on the extent of damage suffered by residents, including to homes, belongings, and other facilities. It was earlier reported that the storm affected more than 200 houses across five residential areas after nearly two hours of continuous heavy rain and strong winds.

items.title: Don't force children into boarding schools, says Mara chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parents should not force their children to enrol in boarding schools, including Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM), if they show no interest, said Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. The Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman warned that such coercion could severely impact a child's emotional well-being and development. "Do not force them if the child has no interest in entering boarding schools, including MRSM.

items.title: Tok Mat: Fund for Palestine audited, money given to UN, Red Crescent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Money collected via the Humanitarian Trust Fund for the People of Palestine is audited and distributed to international organisations operating in the war-torn area, the Foreign Ministry said. Its minister, Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, said the trust fund was managed transparently and in accordance with established procedures. "Our NGO is Ops Ihsan, which was established by the ministry and has 38 affiliated bodies, including Mercy Malaysia and we ensure that aid really reaches there.

items.title: Highway 'race' involving Hilux and Viva under police investigation
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Police are investigating a viral video showing two vehicles allegedly being driven recklessly on the North-South Expressway in Seberang Perai Selatan here. District deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Norazmi Abd Ghapar said police detected the two-minute and 43-second video on Facebook at about 8.30pm yesterday. He said the district police would continue to intensify traffic enforcement operations, including Op Mabuk, to ensure road safety.

items.title: Russia says US drifting from role as 'objective mediator' in Ukraine war
items.summary: MOSCOW: Russia on Tuesday said the United States was no longer trying to be an "objective mediator" in its efforts to broker an end to the Ukraine war, launched by Moscow's offensive in 2022. US-led talks on ending Europe's deadliest conflict since World War II remain effectively frozen as President Donald Trump has shifted his attention towards the Middle East after ordering strikes on Iran in late February. As well as increasing supplies of air defence equipment to Ukraine, the leaders agreed to increase the pressure on the Russian "war economy" by strengthening sanctions, including on Moscow's fossil fuel revenues.

items.title: 'Digital mob mentality' targeting refugees becoming a real-world problem, say groups
items.summary: The Communications and Multimedia Content Forum of Malaysia has urged social media platforms to address algorithms pushing rage-bait content that repeatedly fuels hostility towards refugees and other vulnerable communities. She said platforms must also go beyond removing illegal content and address systems that reward harmful engagement. "If harmful content is repeatedly amplified because it performs well, platforms must respond to that risk," she said.

items.title: Sabah authorities investigate video of alleged school hostel bullying
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — Authorities are investigating a widely shared video allegedly showing a girl being bullied at a school hostel in Sabah’s interior district of Tenom. Police and the Sabah Education Department have opened investigations into the 30-second video, which shows a young girl crouching and crying while another girl stands over her, gestures at her and later messes with her hair. Sabah Education Department director Datuk Raisin Saidin also said the department was aware of the video and was looking into the matter.

items.title: UN chief urges AI firms to 'come clean' over environmental footprint
items.summary: PARIS: UN chief Antonio Guterres called Tuesday for faster action on global warming, challenging AI firms to "come clean" about their environmental footprint and warning that fossil fuels were driving climate and energy crises. Guterres announced new initiatives to combat methane emissions and address concerns over the environmental footprint of energy-hungry data centres. "It is time to come clean," Guterres said.

items.title: Taman Negara's Seberang Ara canopy walkway reopens after repairs
items.summary: JERANTUT: Taman Negara's popular 700m Seberang Ara canopy walkway has reopened to visitors after being closed for more than a year due to structural damage caused by fallen trees. The 700m-long walkway, one of the park's most popular attractions, had remained closed for repair works since February last year. "The canopy walkway is one of the main attractions for visitors to the national park.

items.title: Israel's 'deliberate targeting' of children part of ongoing Gaza 'genocide': UN probe
items.summary: GENEVA: Israel is deliberately targeting Palestinian children in what has become a key factor in an ongoing "genocide" in Gaza, United Nations investigators charged on Tuesday, in a report slammed by Israel. The UN Independent International Commission of Inquiry said it had found evidence that "Palestinian children have been deliberately targeted and killed by Israeli security forces." This, it said, was a key factor in establishing "the genocidal intent of the Israeli authorities and security forces to destroy the larger Palestinian group in Gaza."

items.title: ‘Look after your own rice bowl’: Masidi urges Sabahans to report illegal diesel sales
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — Sabahans have been urged to “be more nationalistic” by reporting any illegal sale of subsidised diesel, as part of efforts to curb leakages and ensure fuel subsidies reach only eligible recipients. Deputy Chief Minister II Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said a sense of responsibility towards the state, as well as local patriotism, was crucial to ensure the improved targeted diesel subsidy scheme in Sabah achieves its intended objectives. “Please report anyone found illegally selling subsidised diesel to those who are not eligible.

items.title: Pakatan’s Puteri Wangsa hopeful Maszlee says yes to face Onn Hafiz in debate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Former Education Minister Maszlee Malik has openly accepted an invitation to debate with Johor Menteri Besar Onn Hafiz Ghazi ahead of the upcoming Johor state election. Maszlee, who was announced as Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, said he accepted the invitation out of respect for democracy and his belief that debates could serve as a platform to foster greater political maturity among the people. The proposal to organise a “Johor State Election Grand Debate”, expected to be among the highlights of the election campaign, was mooted by social media influencer Muhamad Harris Nasril and subsequently shared on the Redaksi.com Facebook page, after PH chairman Anwar Ibrahim announced all 56 PH candidates in Muar yesterday.

items.title: 13 plead not guilty to rioting in Jinjang Utara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirteen men, including two teenagers, pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate's Court to rioting with dangerous weapons in Jinjang Utara two weeks ago. T. Kavilan, 25, Wong Kui Yoong, 51, Leow Mun Yee, 33, Leong Chun Kit, 26, Yam Kit Lun, 21, Lim Zhen Huang, 31, Tan Kai Hao, 30, Teh Jin Huat, 40, Wong Kai Yan, 24, Lim Eng Hoon, 24, Kong Wai Lun, 22, Tan Hwa Yuan, 19, and a 17-year-old entered their pleas before Magistrate Illi Marisqa Khalizan. The charge was framed under Section 148 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which carries a maximum five-year jail term, a fine, or both upon conviction.

items.title: Hamzah: Wawasan Negara will strengthen PN's reform agenda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wawasan Negara's participation will add value to Perikatan Nasional's strength, said Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. Hamzah said Parti Cinta Malaysia's (PCM) special delegates' meeting on Saturday had agreed to rebrand the party as Wawasan Negara. Yesterday, PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar announced that the coalition had accepted PCM and Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang) into PN following an emergency Supreme Council meeting.

items.title: Perikatan’s newest component Parti Wawasan not given Johor seats, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Perikatan Nasional’s newest component Parti Wawasan Negara has not been given any seat to contest under the coalition in the upcoming Johor state election, PN deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man reportedly said today. According to Malaysiakini , Tuan Ibrahim said negotiations for all 56 Johor state seats currently involve only PAS, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP). Asked whether Bersatu would contest under the PN banner in Johor, Tuan Ibrahim reportedly replied: “Yes.

items.title: 'Muda-PSM alliance built on reform, anti-corruption agenda'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muda's alliance with Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) under the Progressive Bloc was built on a shared commitment to institutional reform, anti-corruption and the people's economy. Muda president Amira Aisya Abd Aziz said PSM had been Muda's closest ally since 2023 in pushing for clean institutions and speaking up for ordinary Malaysians. On Thursday, Amira said Muda would contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections under the Progressive Bloc with PSM.

items.title: As Sungai Nibong bows out, Perda pitches Tasek Gelugor for Pesta Pulau Pinang
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR, June 23 — The Penang state government has been urged to consider relocating Pesta Pulau Pinang to land owned by the Penang Regional Development Authority (Perda) following the announcement that the 2025/2026 edition will be the last to be held at the Sungai Nibong fairgrounds near here, before a six-year hiatus beginning next year. Its chairman Datuk Dr Shaik Hussein Mydin said the proposal was aimed at identifying an alternative venue for the event, which benefits local entrepreneurs, adding that Perda-owned land would be more suitable in terms of size and accessibility. Also present were Rashidi, who is also Perda deputy chairman, and Perda general manager Datuk Mohd Asri Baharum.

items.title: Man seriously injured in alleged stabbing by wife in Kota Samarahan
items.summary: Acting Kota Samarahan district police chief Deputy Superintendent Barry William said the 35-year-old victim suffered severe abdominal injuries in the 6.50pm incident. He said police were alerted to the incident by hospital authorities, who reported treating a man believed to have been stabbed by his wife. "The suspect claimed she acted in self-defence after being assaulted by the victim," Barry said.

items.title: Health Ministry committed to sustaining private clinic operations - Dzulkefly
items.summary: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said such intervention measures are critical to building a healthy healthcare ecosystem, while safeguarding the welfare of private medical practitioners. In her supplementary question, Halimah said that the private primary healthcare sector is currently under pressure due to a decline in the intake of house officers, with 2,034 private medical clinics reportedly having closed since 2013. "The primary healthcare system, comprising 2,916 MOH health clinics and 10,208 private GP clinics, is the backbone and foundation of our basic healthcare system," he said.

items.title: Monk's RM1 million win a reminder to use money wisely
items.summary: Chan Chai Siang, the second BSN Sijil Simpanan Premium (SSP) millionaire from Penang this year, said he did not save with the intention of winning, but only to keep his money safe. Chan said he only believed the news after BSN representatives personally met him. I will use it when needed," he said.

items.title: Latin America's resurgent right notches another win in Colombia
items.summary: POPULIST leaders are sweeping into power across Latin America, with Colombian voters the latest to embrace hardline right-wing politics – but will the trend last? Latin America's presidential systems can facilitate this trend of outsider candidates, according to Zanotti, a researcher at Budapest's CEU Democracy Institute and Santiago's Ultra-Lab. "In the 2000s we saw 'pink tide' governments in Latin America" with the left winning many elections," said Anthony Pereira of Tulane University, who sees populism as an evolving tradition in Latin America.

items.title: MACC officers ordered to update asset declarations within one month
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the move was initiated by the new MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as a lead-by-example approach for the agency's personnel. She said that under the new leadership, MACC is focusing on restoring its image and public trust through five key principles, namely, uncompromising integrity, fair enforcement, prevention over punishment, technology and transparency; and building public confidence. The report placed MACC's integrity and its then leadership, Tan Sri Azam Baki, under intense public scrutiny.

items.title: Durian glut hits Penang: Exporters face high costs, dumping drives prices down despite king’s demand
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR, June 23 — The Penang state government is trying to help local durian exporters to deal with the dumping of the king of fruits that has occurred following constraints in exporting to overseas markets. State Trade, Entrepreneurship and Rural Development Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said he himself had held several meetings with the Penang Durian Entrepreneurs Association to discuss the issue, including logistical challenges and high transportation costs. Rashidi said the state government together with the Penang State Agriculture Department are discussing to open up more space for exporters who face obstacles in bringing local durian products to overseas markets.

items.title: Meth-fuelled motorcyclist crashes into hospital's glass doors
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A 28-year-old motorcyclist, who later tested positive for methamphetamine, sustained minor injuries after crashing through the glass doors of the Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital emergency unit. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the rider lost control of his vehicle while entering the hospital's Green Zone at 3am, before ploughing into the glass doors. Azli said the man suffered light injuries to both hands and was treated at the emergency unit.

items.title: 1,059 hiking accidents, 63 fatalities recorded since 2021 - Syed Ibrahim
items.summary: Deputy Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh said statistics from the Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia highlighted the need to strengthen safety measures amid the growing popularity of hiking and forest recreational activities in recent years. "The Forestry Department has also made it mandatory to engage Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP) in 189 high-risk hiking areas. These certified guides support hiker safety, emergency response and compliance with hiking practices.

items.title: Fatal 6-vehicle crash: Trailer driver died after being thrown onto road
items.summary: KULAI: A trailer driver was killed after being thrown from his vehicle in a six-vehicle crash that sparked a major fire and crippled traffic along the North-South Expressway here today. A Johor Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said the 9.02am incident involved two trailers, a Toyota Hilux, a Nissan Vanette, a 10-tonne lorry transporting tiles and a one-tonne lorry. The spokesman said four of the vehicles were destroyed after catching fire in the collision, which paralysed southbound traffic for several hours.

items.title: From syabu to fentanyl, synthetic scourge fuels 70pc of Malaysia’s 192,000 abuse cases, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Synthetic drug abuse in the country continues to present a worrying situation, with 141,817 cases recorded last year, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said 145,526 cases were recorded in 2023, followed by 192,857 cases in 2024. “Synthetic drugs are the most widely abused substances in the country, accounting for about 70 per cent of all drug abuse cases, particularly involving Amphetamine-Type Stimulants (ATS),” he said during Ministers’ Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Up to one billion litres of diesel to be saved every year by MyKad subsidy plan, says finance minister II
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — The federal government’s new MyKad-based targeted diesel subsidy system is expected to curb abuse and smuggling in the subsidised fuel supply chain, with authorities estimating savings of up to one billion litres of diesel annually. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the new mechanism, which takes effect in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan on July 1, would link subsidised diesel purchases directly to eligible Malaysian citizens through their MyKad. Amir Hamzah said each eligible individual would receive a basic monthly quota of 200 litres for subsidised fuel purchases, covering both RON95 petrol and diesel.

items.title: Government lights up highways: RM25m, 3,000 solar poles, 32 black spots set for safety upgrade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The government has allocated RM25.2 million for the initial phase of installing solar-powered LED street lights at 32 high-risk and accident-prone locations along highway networks nationwide. Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the initiative, under the Madani Monitoring programme, involves the installation of 3,000 light poles to enhance road users’ safety at the identified locations. Also present were Works Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Azman Ibrahim, LLM chairman Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, LLM director-general Datuk Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff and Public Works Department director-general Datuk Ibrahim Esa.

items.title: PDRM vows firm action, says Interpol help on table to combat World Cup betting syndicates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) is prepared to seek assistance from international law enforcement agencies if necessary to track down illegal gambling activities or match-fixing syndicates linked to the Fifa World Cup 2026 involving transnational criminal networks. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said that although current monitoring efforts are focused on syndicates operating within the country, PDRM remains ready to expand cooperation with foreign enforcement counterparts, including Aseanapol and Interpol. A total of 42 websites used to promote illegal gambling activities were also detected during the operation.

items.title: Police open disciplinary probe after personnel goes viral in China, IGP warns every officer accountable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has opened a disciplinary investigation into one of its personnel who recently went viral over allegations of insulting local residents while travelling in China. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the personnel concerned remained on active duty pending the outcome of the investigation. Earlier, Mohd Khalid officiated the 42nd Annual General Meeting of the Retired Senior Police Officers Association of Malaysia (RESPA) at the Kuala Lumpur Police Training College here.

items.title: Lorry driver who saved 21 people near Menora Tunnel awarded RM10,000 by national council
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — A lorry driver has received a RM10,000 cash award from the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) after helping stop a runaway bus near the Menora Tunnel on June 15, saving 21 passengers. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the recognition was given to Wong Tuck Meng for his life-saving intervention, which prevented what could have been a major tragedy. Truck driver Wong Tuck Meng, who was honoured for helping slow a runaway bus near the Menora Tunnel, speaks to the press at Parliament in Kuala Lumpur, June 23, 2026.

items.title: No room for excuses: Salleh says every Pan Borneo delay drains Sabah’s coffers and patience
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — Inspector General of Projects Sabah Datuk Seri Dr Salleh Said Keruak reaffirmed his commitment to closely monitoring the implementation of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project, stressing that every delay carries a cost to both government expenditure and public benefit. Speaking after a site visit on Tuesday with officials from the Public Works Department (JKR), contractors and relevant agencies, Salleh said several key work packages (WP) under the project were making encouraging progress and remained on track to achieve major milestones this year. WP 3, 4, 10 and 29 are on track to obtain their Certificates of Partial Completion (CPC) this year,” he said.

items.title: PCM rebrands as Parti Wawasan Negara, Hamzah says move boosts Perikatan’s strength and appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Parti Cinta Malaysia (PCM) has officially rebranded itself as Parti Wawasan Negara, with its de facto leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin saying the move marks a new chapter for both the party and Perikatan Nasional (PN). In a statement today, Hamzah said delegates at a special party meeting on June 20 unanimously approved the name change to Parti Wawasan Negara. “The inclusion of Wawasan Negara also adds value to Perikatan Nasional’s existing strengths, particularly in advancing an agenda of change founded on competence, integrity and stability,” he said.

items.title: Education Ministry orders immediate school interventions for students showing mental health warning signs
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 23 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) has urged schools to carry out immediate interventions for students identified as showing signs of mental health issues to ensure their welfare and safety continue to be safeguarded. “We conduct mental health screening interventions, while efforts to strengthen the capacity of school counsellors are also being given due attention. Meanwhile, she said the Safe School Management Guidelines and the School Student Protection Policy introduced by the MOE are mandatory for all school administrators to implement without compromise.

items.title: Perak Education Dept cooperates with police probe after Form One student critically injured in school fall
items.summary: IPOH, June 23 — The Perak State Education Department (JPN) is fully cooperating with police in the investigation into an incident involving a Form One student who was reported to have fallen from a building at a school yesterday. “The student is currently receiving treatment in hospital. “JPN Perak has taken the necessary measures to ensure the student’s family receives appropriate welfare assistance and support.

items.title: Hiking boom turns deadly with 63 fatalities and over 1,000 accidents, govt moves to tighten trail safety
items.summary: Deputy Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh said statistics from the Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia (JBPM) highlighted the need to strengthen safety measures amid the growing popularity of hiking and forest recreational activities in recent years. “JPSM has also made it mandatory to engage Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP) in 189 high-risk hiking areas. These certified guides support hiker safety, emergency response and compliance with hiking practices.

items.title: Iranian ambassador highlights 60-day oil waiver as chance for Petronas and Malaysia to deepen cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The 60-day waiver on the sale of Iranian crude oil in the global market has opened the door for Iran and Malaysia to further strengthen economic cooperation, particularly in the energy sector. Iranian Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said the waiver allows countries to purchase Iranian crude oil and petroleum products without restrictions, creating opportunities for deeper collaboration. “Any country now can buy oil and oil production from Iran, and there is no ban on such cooperation.

items.title: Fadhlina Sidek demands stricter school safety, guidelines enforced after student’s fatal fall in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 23 — The Education Ministry has ordered all schools nationwide to strengthen their protection and safety aspects through the implementation of mandatory school security guidelines without any compromise. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the ministry has taken drastic steps to address protection and safety issues in schools by introducing mandatory security guidelines that were announced last week. “The aspect of student protection and safety must be given serious attention by schools to ensure a safe and conducive learning environment for all.

items.title: ‘We won’t be a rubber stamp’: MPs demand parliamentary vetting for public prosecutor in constitutional split Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Several PKR lawmakers today called on the government to expand parliamentary oversight in the planned constitutional amendments aimed at separating the offices of the Attorney General (AG) and the Public Prosecutor (PP). Among the points of contention cited is Clause 18 of the amendment, which the lawmakers said only requires the Judicial and Legal Service Commission to inform Parliament of nominated names and allow for comments. “What we must have is a bipartisan parliamentary vetting process for nominees proposed by the Judicial and Legal Service Commission, with open and transparent hearings in which Parliament on behalf of the rakyat, is able to rigorously examine nominees,” he said.

items.title: Rival parties cannot use same coalition logo in same seat, says EC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Election Commission has reportedly said election candidates from rival parties cannot use the same party or coalition logo when standing in the same constituency. Berita Harian reported that EC deputy chairman Azmi Sharom said the use of a logo is limited to candidates who have been authorised by the party or coalition concerned via a formal letter. According to the report, Former EC deputy chairman Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Wan Omar said Regulation 11(2)(d) of the Election (Conduct of Elections) Regulations bars candidates from different parties from using the same logo.

items.title: SPAN investigates drowning of maintenance worker at Saujana Perdana tower, safety checks under spotlight
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 23 — The National Water Services Commission (SPAN) is conducting investigations into a fatal accident involving a maintenance worker at the Saujana 1 water tower in Kuala Selangor on June 16. In a statement today, the commission said any party found to have failed to comply with required procedures, including Air Selangor or a SPAN permit holder, would face appropriate action. Any infringement of the Water Services Industry Act 2006 (Act 655) or relevant subsidiary legislation will be subject to appropriate action, SPAN said.

items.title: Malaysia must keep engaging US on tariffs, says Miti
items.summary: According to Berita Harian , Johari told the Dewan Rakyat that halting engagement with the US could expose Malaysia to risks and affect the country’s economy and trade. Johari said the additional tariff could make Malaysian products less competitive in the US — a key export market particularly for the E&E and semiconductor sectors. In April, Johari reportedly said Malaysia was facing several US trade actions under various American legal provisions.

items.title: Gombak police tracking man in military-style uniform, suspected replica firearm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Police are tracking down a man dressed in a military-style uniform and carrying what is believed to be a replica firearm, following a video that went viral on social media. Gombak police chief ACP Ibrahim Husin said in a statement today that the incident is believed to have occurred in the vicinity of Taman Bukit Desa, Kepong, Selangor. Members of the public with information related to the incident are urged to contact Insp S. Prakash at 012-294 0827, the Gombak district police headquarters opeQrations room at 03-612 6222, or visit the nearest police station.
